NASMServer - Linux x64 Bundle
==============================

This bundle includes everything needed to run NASMServer on a Linux x64 system
without any additional dependencies.

CONTENTS
--------
  nasmserver            - launcher script (run this)
  nasmserver-bin        - the actual server binary (pre-patched)
  installer             - optional setup/installation script
  libs/                 - bundled runtime libraries
  www/                  - default web directory
  env.example           - example configuration file
  instructions.txt      - this file

IMPORTANT: do not move, delete, or rename any of the files above. The launcher
script expects nasmserver-bin and libs/ to be in the same directory as itself.

USAGE
-----
1. Extract the bundle to a directory of your choice

2. Copy the example config:
     cp env.example .env

3. Run it:
     ./nasmserver


INSTALLATION / UPDATE
---------------------
1. Run the install script:
     sudo ./install

2. Use the installed program:
     nasmserver


CONFIGURATION
-------------
Edit .env to configure the server. All keys are optional.

  PORT            Port to listen on (default: 8080)
                  Note: ports below 1024 require root or CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE.

  DOCUMENT_ROOT   Path to the directory to serve files from (default: .)
                  No trailing slash.

  INDEX_FILE      File served when a directory is requested (default: index.html)

  MAX_REQUESTS    Max simultaneous connections, 1-255 (default: 20)

  SERVER_NAME     Value for the Server: response header (default: NASMServer/ver)

  ERRORDOC_400    Path to a custom error page, relative to DOCUMENT_ROOT.
  ERRORDOC_403    Must start with /. If left empty, headers only are sent.
  ERRORDOC_404
  ERRORDOC_405

Note: .env must use LF line endings, not CRLF.
Note 2: On installs, the configuration file is /opt/nasmserver/config.cfg

To use a custom config file path instead of .env:
  ./nasmserver -e /path/to/config.env

For all supported flags:
  ./nasmserver -h
